articles.title: 10 TRILLION SAMPLES A DAY: SCALING BEYOND TRADITIONAL MONITORING INFRA AT DATABRICKS
articles.summary: Databricks rearchitected its monitoring infrastructure to handle over 10 trillion samples daily and five billion active timeseries by transitioning to a customized timeseries database called Pantheon, which has self-healing and efficient tiered storage. This new architecture also uses an advanced aggregation pipeline to manage metric cardinality and a Lakehouse-native platform named Hydra for high-cardinality troubleshooting and 50 times cheaper data storage.

articles.title: MONITORING RELIABLY AT SCALE
articles.summary: Reliable observability at Airbnb requires running monitoring workloads on dedicated, isolated clusters to prevent circular dependencies and maintain visibility during production failures. Furthermore, Airbnb separated telemetry from the standard service mesh and implemented a meta-monitoring layer with a dead man's switch to make sure that the observability stack itself remains resilient against network outages and silent failures.

articles.title: COMPUTER USE IS 45X MORE EXPENSIVE THAN STRUCTURED APIS
articles.summary: A benchmark study shows that AI agents using computer vision to navigate web interfaces are 45 times more expensive than those using structured APIs, largely due to higher token consumption and performance variance. While vision-based agents are still necessary for third-party software, auto-generating API surfaces for internal tools provides a far more efficient and cost-effective deployment strategy.

articles.title: AI DIDN'T DELETE YOUR DATABASE, YOU DID
articles.summary: The accidental deletion of a production database by an AI agent, which was blamed on technology, actually highlighted human oversight and a flaw in the system's architecture (a destructive public-facing API). Current AI models are unpredictable and must be viewed as assistants, requiring human accountability and a deep understanding of the code.

articles.title: WHEN EVERYONE HAS AI AND THE COMPANY STILL LEARNS NOTHING
articles.summary: While many organizations gain individual productivity from AI adoption, these gains often fail to translate into broader organizational learning because traditional corporate structures are too slow to capture informal employee discoveries. Companies must shift from monitoring basic token usage to tracking decision-making improvements, building feedback mechanisms that transform individual AI experiments into shared organizational capabilities.

articles.title: STOP STITCHING DATABASES FOR AI AGENTS
articles.summary: Oracle AI Database acts as a unified memory core for agents [10]. Vector search, relational, JSON, and graph data live together so agents can reason over live enterprise data without extra vector stores, pipelines, or synchronization jobs.

articles.title: SEE HOW DEVELOPERS BUILD AGENT MEMORY →
AGENTIC INBOX
articles.summary: Agentic Inbox is a self-hosted email client and AI assistant running on Cloudflare's serverless infrastructure, using Durable Objects and R2 for storage to make sure communication data and attachments remain under the user's direct control. The system includes a built-in AI agent that generates draft replies requiring manual approval.

articles.title: AIRBYTE AGENTS
articles.summary: Airbyte Agents is a data and context layer for AI agents. It gives your agents real-time access to business data through open-source, type-safe connectors, managed credentials, and low-latency search.

articles.title: BUILDING A LEANER AND FASTER COINBASE
articles.summary: Coinbase is reducing its workforce by about 14% to navigate a down market and integrate the efficiency gains provided by AI. The organization plans to flatten its structure to a maximum of five layers, requiring all leaders to act as both managers and active individual contributors.

articles.title: THE REAL COST OF REACT NATIVE ANIMATIONS: BENCHMARKING EVERY APPROACH
articles.summary: Benchmarking various React Native animation libraries show that standard approaches often consume the frame budget through repetitive shadow tree commits, leading to performance stutters. Native-driven solutions eliminate this overhead by offloading animations to system render servers, while worklet-based libraries are necessary for complex, gesture-driven interactions.

articles.title: ASYNC RUST NEVER LEFT THE MVP STATE
articles.summary: Async Rust currently introduces binary bloat on microcontrollers because compiler-generated state machines are not yet true zero-cost abstractions. A formal project goal and funding are being sought to integrate compiler optimizations that collapse identical states and remove unnecessary overhead, so that these abstractions can be brought closer to their intended efficiency.

articles.title: THREE INVERSE LAWS OF AI AND ROBOTICS
articles.summary: The "Three Inverse Laws of Robotics" framework protects society from uncritical acceptance of generative AI by prohibiting anthropomorphism and blind trust in machine output, asserting that humans must remain fully accountable for AI-driven outcomes.

articles.title: GOOGLE CHROME SILENTLY INSTALLS A 4 GB AI MODEL ON YOUR DEVICE WITHOUT CONSENT
articles.summary: Google Chrome is reportedly installing the 4 GB Gemini Nano AI model on millions of devices without user consent.

articles.title: 10 LESSONS FOR AGENTIC CODING
articles.summary: Devs should use AI for fast implementation and automation while focusing their human expertise on testing, documentation, and high-value architectural decisions.

articles.title: CONSTRUCT WITH COLLABORATORS, CALL WITH WORK
articles.summary: Pass an object's stable, identity-defining dependencies (collaborators) to its constructor and its variable, per-operation parameters (work) to its methods.

articles.title: ACCELERATING GEMMA 4: FASTER INFERENCE WITH MULTI-TOKEN PREDICTION DRAFTERS
articles.summary: New Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) drafters for Gemma 4 models use a speculative decoding architecture to achieve up to a 3x faster inference for developers without any degradation in output quality or reasoning logic.
